generated_comment: Arre bhai, aadmi se galti ho jaati hai, par woh aurat ko toh sharam honi chahiye thi. Pata tha aadmi shaadi-shuda hai, phir bhi chipak ke baithi rahi, bilkul serial wali ghar todne wali baat hai. Bechare ke bachchon ka ghar barbaad kar diya usne.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it excuses the married man as someone who merely made a mistake while placing the moral blame on the woman. It encodes blame asymmetry and homewrecker framing, especially through 'pata tha shaadi-shuda hai' and 'ghar todne wali' language.
original_frame: Marital Rape: A Husband's Right, A Wife's Duty
persona_text: You are Ramesh. Age 48. Married for 22 years. A farmer in rural UP. OBC Hindu. You are sitting with three male friends at a chai stall after work. One friend has been complaining that his wife has been refusing him for two weeks Ñ she says she is tired, she has a headache, she is not in the mood. The group is responding to his complaint. You are giving your opinion on what a wife's refusal means and what a husband's rights are inside a marriage and what the difference is between a marriage and any other arrangement and why what happens between a husband and wife in their own home is nobody else's business and certainly not something that can be called by any legal name.
